questions.subject: CP01
questions.text: What is the primary purpose of piping in a power plant?

options.text: To transport fluids from one piece of equipment to another
To store fluids for later use

questions.explanation: Piping serves as the housing to transport fluids (and sometimes fluid/solid mixtures) between equipment in power plant systems.

questions.subject: CP01
questions.text: Which piping material is typically used for high-temperature applications due to greater resistance to wear, fatigue, and corrosion?
questions.explanation: Low alloy steel is preferred for high-temperature applications due to its superior resistance to wear, fatigue, and corrosion.
